footer.disclaimer: Miruwa flags monitoring risks for professional review. It does not diagnose or prescribe.

landing.heroSub: Daily WhatsApp check-ins capture weight, blood pressure, and missed medications. Deterministic rules surface only the patients who need review — so nursing time shifts from scanning charts to acting on what matters.
landing.safetyLine: Monitoring support only. Not diagnosis. No treatment recommendation.

proof.label: Built for hospital evaluation
proof.item3: Every escalation cites its rule + data

glimpse.title: One queue. Only the exceptions.
glimpse.sub: A nurse doesn't scan 24 patients to find the 3 who need review. Miruwa surfaces escalations first, and every one cites the exact rule (HF-001: weight +2 kg in 3 days) and the patient's real data — not a black box, fully auditable.
glimpse.row2Note: BP high two mornings in a row
glimpse.stableLine: 21 stable patients — checked automatically, nothing to do

preview.label: Patient · Miruwa
preview.bubbleText: Feeling more tired today… my legs look swollen.
preview.name: Mrs. Chan, 78

problem.title: The gap between visits is where risk lives.
problem.sub: Patients with chronic conditions like heart failure and hypertension can deteriorate in the weeks between appointments. Small early signals — weight up 2 kg in three days, missed medications, persistent swelling — quietly become crises. No nurse can manually check every patient, every day.
problem.step2Body: Days to weeks pass with little visibility.
problem.step4Body: Condition worsens, costs go up, stress rises.
problem.bottleneck1: Elderly patients may not open new apps or remember daily check-ins.
problem.bottleneck2: Families and care teams have limited visibility.
problem.bottleneck3: Delays can lead to avoidable deterioration and urgent care.

how.title: From a WhatsApp message to a nurse decision.
how.sub: Four steps. No app for the patient to install, no manual data entry for your team.
how.step1Body: A daily Cantonese, English, or Arabic check-in. No app to install, no login to forget.
how.step2Body: Symptoms, weight, blood pressure, and voice notes become structured data.
how.step3Body: Explainable rules (HF-001, not a black box) decide what needs attention. No AI diagnosis — ever.
how.step4Body: An exception-first dashboard with a full audit trail. One nurse covers a whole ward.

voice.title: Voice in. Understood.
voice.sub: An elderly patient records a voice message in their own language over WhatsApp. Miruwa transcribes it, structures the symptoms for the nurse, and replies in the patient's language.
voice.claim: Miruwa transcribes voice notes in ~100 languages and replies in the patient's language — English, 繁體中文, العربية today.

features.title: Built for clinical accountability.
features.sub: Not engagement metrics — the exports, alerts, and audit trail a monitoring program actually runs on.
features.f1Body: Conservative, explainable rules with rule IDs. Every escalation says exactly why.
features.f2Body: Cantonese, English, and Arabic over WhatsApp — the channel elderly patients already use.
features.f3Body: Nurses review the patients who need it, not all of them.
features.f4Body: Family is looped in on escalations, with nurse-controlled wording.
features.f5Body: Weekly summaries, PDF reports, and FHIR-style export into existing workflows.
features.f6Body: Every access and action recorded. Built for clinical accountability.

trust.item2: Encrypted in transit and at rest
trust.honesty: Not yet SOC 2 certified — we're early and say so. Security documentation is shared with every evaluating hospital.

cta.title: See Miruwa live on your ward's workflow.
cta.sub: A 30-minute session with our team — your patient mix, your languages, your escalation rules. No pitch deck, just the product, live.

business.title: Business case
business.sub: Miruwa turns between-visit monitoring into an operational workflow. Here is who buys it, why, and how it stays safe at scale.

pricing.title: Pricing that follows the engagement
pricing.sub: Miruwa is deployed per hospital, not per seat. We scope every engagement together — here is how it usually runs.
pricing.pilot1: One ward or care program, with a defined patient cohort
pricing.pilot2: Three months, fixed pilot fee scoped to your cohort
pricing.pilot3: Full product: WhatsApp check-ins, risk rules, nurse dashboard
pricing.pilot4: Joint success criteria and weekly review with our team
pricing.standard1: Per-patient, per-month subscription after a successful pilot
pricing.standard2: Unlimited nurse and admin seats — we never charge per clinician
pricing.standard3: Multilingual check-ins, caregiver alerts, PDF + FHIR-style export
pricing.standard4: Email support and product updates included
pricing.enterprise1: Dedicated instance: your own database, application, and WhatsApp number
pricing.enterprise2: Data residency in your preferred region — nothing shared with other hospitals
pricing.enterprise3: Custom escalation thresholds and SLAs; SSO available on request
pricing.enterprise4: Optional self-hosted deployment inside your network
pricing.whyBody: Cohort size, language mix, and integration depth change the economics. We'd rather scope honestly in 30 minutes than publish a number that's wrong for your ward.

security.title: Security & trust
security.sub: How Miruwa protects patient data, in plain language. Questions we haven't answered here: ask us directly.
security.isolationBody: Every production hospital runs its own dedicated Miruwa instance — its own database, its own application deployment, its own WhatsApp number. Your patients' data never shares infrastructure with another organization. Not "logically separated" — physically separate.
security.accessBody: Staff accounts are provisioned by your administrator — there is no public signup. Two roles (administrator and nurse), and every page and API call re-checks the session on the server.
security.encryptionBody: TLS 1.2+ in transit. AES-256 at rest in the managed Postgres database. Service credentials never reach the browser.
security.auditBody: Every access and action is recorded and attributed to the signed-in user — exportable for internal review.
security.deterministicBody: Escalation severity is decided by explainable, versioned rules — never by an AI model. AI is used only to structure language (transcription, wording), never to judge clinical risk.
security.residencyBody: Each dedicated instance is provisioned in your preferred cloud region. (Our public demo runs in the US; production instances are placed where you need them.)
security.backupsBody: Daily automated backups are standard; point-in-time recovery is available on enterprise instances.
security.certsBody: Miruwa is not yet SOC 2 or ISO 27001 certified. We are early, and we won't pretend otherwise. Our roadmap: SOC 2 Type I, then Type II. We will share our security questionnaire answers and architecture documentation with any evaluating hospital.
security.reportingBody: Report security issues privately to the team — never through public issue trackers, and never with real patient data in the report.
security.ctaSub: We'll walk your IT and data-protection teams through everything.

contact.title: Request a demo
contact.sub: Tell us about your ward or program. A real person replies within one business day — no automated sequences.
contact.point1: A 30-minute walkthrough, on your use case
contact.point3: Pilots, pricing, and data-isolation questions all welcome

form.successBody: We'll reply to {email} within one business day.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.s1p: Miruwa provides monitoring support for chronic-care programs: it collects daily check-ins and vital readings, applies deterministic escalation rules, and surfaces results to the care team. Miruwa does not diagnose, prescribe, or replace clinical judgment.
privacy.s2p: On behalf of the deploying healthcare organization, Miruwa processes: patient identification details entered by the care team (name, age, conditions, caregiver contact), WhatsApp messages and voice-note transcripts sent to the check-in number, vital readings (weight, blood pressure, heart rate, activity, sleep), and the resulting risk evaluations. An append-only audit trail records every access and action for accountability.
privacy.s3h: Where it lives and who sees it
privacy.s3p: Data is stored in the organization's dedicated database (Supabase Postgres) and is scoped to that organization. Access requires a staff account provisioned by the organization's administrator. Voice notes are transcribed and free-text messages are structured using AI services (speech-to-text and language models); these services process message content only and never decide clinical severity.
privacy.s4p: The deploying organization controls retention and deletion. Patients and caregivers can direct questions, correction requests, or deletion requests to their care provider, who administers the data. Hong Kong's Personal Data (Privacy) Ordinance (PDPO) applies to deployments in Hong Kong.
privacy.s5p: For privacy questions about a specific deployment, contact the deploying organization's administrator.

terms.title: Terms of use
terms.s1p: Miruwa is monitoring support software. It flags monitoring signals for professional review using deterministic, auditable rules. It does not diagnose, prescribe, or provide treatment recommendations, and it is not a substitute for clinical judgment or emergency services. In an emergency, call local emergency services immediately.
terms.s2p: The nurse dashboard and patient records are restricted to staff accounts provisioned by the deploying organization's administrator. Accounts are personal; credentials must not be shared. The organization is responsible for revoking access when staff leave.
terms.s3p: Daily check-ins depend on third-party messaging (WhatsApp via Twilio) and patient response; the care team remains responsible for following up on missed check-ins. Miruwa is provided as part of a service agreement with the deploying organization, which governs uptime, support, and liability.
terms.s4p: Use of patient data is governed by the deploying organization's agreements and the

onboard.step3Body: Tapping the button links their number, and their record appears on your dashboard to review and confirm.
onboard.disclaimer: Miruwa is monitoring support — not a diagnosis or treatment tool.

settings.title: Settings
settings.sub: Manage your organization's team and configuration.

notifications.title: Alert notifications & SLA chain
notifications.sub: Who gets paged when the deterministic engine raises an alert, and what happens if nobody acknowledges it.
notifications.alertsEmailHint: Ward duty inbox paged on new or worsening alerts
notifications.adminEmailHint: Notified when an alert stays unacknowledged for two full windows
notifications.minSeverityHint: Minimum severity that triggers a page
notifications.chainNote: The chain: page on alert → re-page if unacknowledged past the window → notify the escalation contact after two windows. Every step is recorded in the audit trail.

rules.title: Monitoring rules
rules.sub: The exact deterministic rules that decide severity. No AI in this decision — same data in, same severity out.
rules.note: Every alert stores the engine and threshold versions that produced it, and every rule match carries its data evidence. Changes ship as a new version — historical alerts stay traceable.

editor.title: Tune thresholds
editor.sub: Adjust sensitivity inside clinical guardrails. Rule structure and severities are fixed; every save creates a new audited version.
editor.notePlaceholder: Why this change? (shown in the version history)

rules.HF-001: Rapid weight gain can indicate fluid retention in heart-failure monitoring.
rules.HF-002: Weight gain combined with shortness of breath and swelling requires review.
rules.MED-001: Repeated missed medication increases chronic-care risk.
rules.BP-001: Very high blood pressure should be reviewed urgently.
rules.ACT-001: Reduced activity may signal deterioration or frailty risk.
rules.SYM-001: Patient reported symptoms that warrant nurse review, regardless of condition.
rules.NR-001: A missed daily check-in can hide deterioration — silence is a signal.
rules.NR-002: Multiple days without a completed check-in require follow-up, especially for patients living alone.

recommendedAction.escalate: Nurse review recommended today and notify family. If symptoms are severe, contact emergency services.
recommendedAction.review_today: Nurse review recommended today. Consider booking a clinic follow-up.
recommendedAction.watch: Continue monitoring. Nurse review if the trend continues.
recommendedAction.stable: No action needed. Continue routine monitoring.

safety.banner: Miruwa does not diagnose or prescribe. It flags monitoring risks for professional review.
safety.note: This alert is not a diagnosis or treatment recommendation. It is a monitoring prompt for professional review.

metadata.rootDescription: Miruwa turns daily WhatsApp check-ins, vital signals, and deterministic escalation rules into a nurse-review workflow for chronic-care patients between clinic visits. Monitoring support — not diagnosis.
metadata.ogDescription: Daily WhatsApp check-ins, deterministic risk rules, and an exception-first nurse dashboard for chronic care between clinic visits.
metadata.landingDescription: Daily WhatsApp check-ins, vital signals, and deterministic rules help nurses monitor chronic-care patients between clinic visits. Monitoring support — not diagnosis.
metadata.businessDescription: Who pays for Miruwa and why: elderly-care operators, home-care agencies, clinics and insurers — KPIs, risks at scale, and mitigations.
metadata.pricingDescription: How Miruwa engagements work: pilot program, per-patient subscription, and dedicated enterprise instances. No public price list — every hospital's scope differs.
metadata.securityDescription: How Miruwa protects patient data: dedicated instances per hospital, encryption, audit trails, and deterministic clinical rules.
metadata.contactDescription: Tell us about your ward or program — pilots, pricing, and security reviews. A real person replies within one business day.
metadata.privacyDescription: How Miruwa handles patient monitoring data.
metadata.termsDescription: Terms of use for the Miruwa monitoring platform.
